methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $5.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.8B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Helion is a Series F climate and energy company founded by David Kirtley and Chris Pihl, currently valued at $5.4B post-money on $425M+ raised. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine same-sector peers spanning similar valuation tiers, including eight private companies (TAE Technologies at $6.0B Series G, Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B Series B, Form Energy at $3.5B Series G) and one public comparable (QuantumScape, $5.0B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical for Series F advancement, implies $7.0B. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company comp set yields $3.5B, with the peer range spanning $1.8B to $6.0B. The dispersion reflects heterogeneity in stage and commercialization maturity within the climate-tech sector.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $3.0B (low) to $8.1B (high), with a median of $5.3B. The current $5.4B valuation sits near the analytical midpoint, consistent with stage-adjusted peer positioning. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-progression multiplier in an illiquid sector and the relevance of earlier-stage comparables (CFS at Series B) to a later-stage fund raise. The wide peer range ($1.8B–$6.0B) underscores valuation sensitivity to technology risk and commercialization timeline assumptions within frontier energy.
